scope: SYBEORG only; no Radi / House of Meroe files, targets, drafts, accounts, or campaigns inspected or used.
researchMethod: Overpass/OSM public listing discovery across Iowa/Nebraska Midwest markets plus direct HTTP checks for selected website hypotheses where useful. No outbound contact was made.

selectedLeads.diagnosis: Drivers searching from a phone see a directory entry instead of a shop-controlled first page with the phone, location, and repair-intake context.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Minimum-useful one-page repair-counter site with tap-to-call, Marion address, directions, and verified service/hours content.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: The ask is concrete and low-risk: they already have a public phone/address, and the mockup makes the missing first page visible without asking them to imagine a full website rebuild.
selectedLeads.firstOffer: $699 starter page or $1,200 fuller local service page after verification of services/photos/hours.
selectedLeads.upsellPath: Add Google Business Profile cleanup, service-specific pages, review/photo reuse, and call tracking for ads.
selectedLeads.designDifferentiation: Light repair-bay work-order board, location-first routing, no dark contractor hero/right quote form pattern.
selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: Local browser screenshot looked specific and non-generic; sample framing visible; sticky mobile phone path present; no fabricated proof.
selectedLeads.draftCopy: Hi — this is Majid with SYBEORG. I was looking at Marion auto repair listings and noticed Master Auto Repair shows a phone and 7th Avenue address, but I did not see an owned website link in the public listing I checked. I made a no-obligation sample first page that puts the phone, location, and repair-intake path up front for mobile searchers. Worth polishing this up for you? If not, no worries — tell me NO and I will not follow up.

publicListingSignals: OSM node/13121679849 shows phone/address but no website field
Direct domain check resolved to Wix site
Fetched HTML showed no form/tel/mailto links in audit pass
selectedLeads.diagnosis: The owned site exists, but the public listing reviewed did not expose it, and the fetched homepage HTML did not show direct tel/form/mailto hooks. That can leak mobile visitors who want to call quickly.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Helpful audit note plus a mobile call-path cleanup: sticky tap-to-call, visible appointment request path, and GMB website-field verification.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: This is framed as fixing a visible listing/contact-path mismatch, not replacing their whole site.
selectedLeads.firstOffer: $300-$600 mobile CTA/GMB cleanup audit or $699 conversion landing page if they want a sample page.
selectedLeads.upsellPath: Appointment-request page, repair-service landing pages, and call tracking if they run ads.
selectedLeads.designDifferentiation: No mockup created; current evidence supports a helpful alert and cleanup offer first.
selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: No mockup QA because no mockup was created. Manual browser review recommended before using site-specific copy.
selectedLeads.draftCopy: Hi — this is Majid with SYBEORG. While checking Waukee auto repair listings, I noticed the public listing I reviewed had Lund Motors' phone/address but did not show the owned website field, even though lundmotors.com is live. I also did not see an obvious tel/form/mailto hook in the fetched homepage HTML, so I would treat this as a quick mobile contact-path cleanup before any bigger redesign talk. Worth having me polish that into a short checklist for you? Reply NO and I will not follow up.

publicListingSignals: OSM node/10657732272 shows phone/address/hours and no website field
Direct check of morrowcollisioncenter.com redirected to morrowcollision.com
Fetched HTML had no title/form/tel/mailto links in first pass
selectedLeads.diagnosis: Potential brand/domain/listing mismatch: public listing does not expose a website field, while a guessed center-specific domain redirects to a different shorter domain with weak machine-readable contact signals in the first fetch.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Helpful alert: confirm official domain/GMB website button, add clear title/contact routes, and make collision estimate path easy on mobile.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: Collision repair is high-ticket and trust-sensitive; a domain/listing mismatch is a specific fix, not a generic website pitch.
selectedLeads.firstOffer: $300 helpful-alert cleanup / GMB + homepage contact-path check.
selectedLeads.upsellPath: Collision estimate landing page, photo upload/request flow, and review/proof reuse once verified.
selectedLeads.designDifferentiation: No mockup created; should first verify the exact official domain and avoid building around a possibly guessed URL.
selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: No mockup created because a domain/listing verification alert is the cleanest first touch.
selectedLeads.draftCopy: Hi — this is Majid with SYBEORG. I was checking collision-repair listings around Lincoln and noticed Morrow Collision Center's public listing did not show a website field in the source I reviewed. A quick domain check also redirected from morrowcollisioncenter.com to morrowcollision.com, and the fetched page did not expose a clear title or tap-to-call/contact hook in my first pass. I wanted to flag it in case the Google/listing website button needs cleanup. Worth me sending the short notes? Reply NO and I will not follow up.

publicListingSignals: OSM node/13986180983 shows website, info@rightroofing.com, +1-515-729-0770, address, and Mo-Fr hours
Homepage title: Right Roofing & Siding | Iowa Roofing Contractor Since 1997
Fetched HTML had no form/tel/mailto links in first pass
selectedLeads.diagnosis: They already have a credible site and proof angle, so the leak is not 'needs website'; it is a dedicated storm/roof-repair ad landing path with trackable calls/forms and a faster mobile CTA.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Storm-damage / roof-repair landing page built to match their existing brand and route paid/local visitors to a measurable call/request path.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: A polished roofing company is more likely to buy a campaign-specific page than a generic rebuild, especially in seasonal storm windows.
selectedLeads.firstOffer: $699-$1,200 campaign landing page with call tracking-ready CTA copy and form routing.
selectedLeads.upsellPath: Google Ads/local service campaign support, storm-damage service pages, tracking dashboard, retargeting creative.
selectedLeads.designDifferentiation: No mockup created; useful version should borrow their existing brand/proof after manual browser review rather than generic roofing graphics.

realAssetsFound.siteTitle: Right Roofing & Siding | Iowa Roofing Contractor Since 1997

selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: No mockup created because quality standard requires brand-specific proof and likely site screenshots/assets first.
selectedLeads.draftSubject: Storm/roof-repair landing page idea for Right Roofing
selectedLeads.draftCopy: Hi Right Roofing team — Majid with SYBEORG here. I was reviewing Iowa roofing sites and noticed your main site already has a strong credibility angle. The opportunity I would not pitch as a rebuild; it looks more like a dedicated storm-damage / roof-repair landing page with one trackable call/request path for high-intent visitors. I can put together a no-obligation sample that matches your current brand instead of a generic contractor template. Worth polishing this up for you? Reply NO and I will not follow up.

publicListingSignals: OSM node/10657969301 shows phone +1-402-202-8677, address 6000 West Adams Street, Mo-Fr 07:00-16:00
selectedLeads.diagnosis: The listing has phone, address, and hours, but no owned website path, so it is a strong profile-completion/local-presence cleanup candidate.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Google/listing cleanup plus a simple shop page that verifies hours/location and puts tap-to-call first.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: The proof burden is low: the public listing already has enough verified facts to make a minimum useful page, but no need to force a mockup before contact route improves.
selectedLeads.firstOffer: $300 profile/contact-path cleanup or $699 starter page if they want a public shop page.
selectedLeads.upsellPath: Repair-service pages, review/photo reuse, and appointment request form after first page.
selectedLeads.designDifferentiation: No mockup created; use listing cleanup angle first to avoid another generic auto-shop page in same batch.
selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: No mockup created; marked research-only to maintain quality and avoid repeating auto-repair template patterns.
selectedLeads.draftCopy: Hi — this is Majid with SYBEORG. I was checking Lincoln auto repair listings and noticed Kallhoff Auto Repair shows a phone, West Adams Street address, and weekday hours, but I did not see an owned website link in the public listing I reviewed. That is usually a small fix: either clean up the listing path or add a simple tap-to-call shop page. Worth me sending the short notes? Reply NO and I will not follow up.

mockupsSkippedReason: Quality standard: skipped generic or insufficiently verified mockups; used helpful-alert/research-only drafts where a mockup would be premature.
